en.extract:
The white-backed thrush or Kessler's thrush (Turdus kessleri) is a species of bird in the family Turdidae. It is primarily resident or a short-distance altitudinal migrant, found in central China in Gansu, Qinghai, Sichuan, eastern Tibet, and northwestern Yunnan, and also a scarce winter visitor in Bhutan, northeasternmost India (Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim), and eastern Nepal. It breeds in alpine scrub on rocky sites at altitudes of 3600–4500 metres, and descends down to 2100 metres, rarely to 1500 metres, in winter.
It is a large thrush, 28 cm long (same size as a mistle thrush). The male has a black head, wings, and tail, and a pink body, dark pink below and pale pink to nearly white on the mantle (thus superficially resembling a rosy starling in plumage pattern, though larger and longer-tailed than that). The female is similar but drabber, with a dark brown head and the pink tinged greyer. Juveniles are similar to adult females, but with pale streaks on the head. It is gregarious, forming flocks particularly in winter, but also on the breeding grounds, and often joins flocks of dusky thrush and red-throated thrush. It is omnivorous, feeding on insects, earthworms, and berries, with juniper cones ("berries") particularly important in winter.

The '''white-backed thrush''' or '''Kessler's thrush''' ('''''Turdus kessleri''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Turdidae]]. It is primarily resident or a short-distance [[bird migration|altitudinal migrant]], found in central [[China]] in [[Gansu]], [[Qinghai]], [[Sichuan]], eastern [[Tibet]], and northwestern [[Yunnan]],<ref name=Thrushes>{{cite book | last1=Clement | first1=Peter | last2=Hathway | first2=Ren | title=Thrushes | publisher=A&C Black | publication-place=London | date=2000-11-30 | isbn=0-7136-3940-7 | pages=36–37, 367–368}}</ref><ref name=China>{{cite book | last1=MacKinnon | first1=John Ramsay | last2=Phillipps | first2=Karen | title=A Field Guide to the Birds of China | publisher=Oxford University Press | publication-place=New York | date=2000-06-08 | isbn=0-19-854940-7 | page=}}</ref> and also a scarce winter visitor in [[Bhutan]], northeasternmost [[India]] ([[Arunachal Pradesh]] and [[Sikkim]]), and eastern [[Nepal]].<ref name=Thrushes/><ref name=India>{{cite book | last1=Kazmierczak | first1=Krys | last2=Perlo | first2=Ber van | title=A field guide to the birds of the Indian subcontinent | publisher=Yale University Press | publication-place=New Haven | date=2000 | isbn=0-300-07921-4 | page=}}</ref> It breeds in [[alpine climate|alpine]] [[shrubland|scrub]] on rocky sites at altitudes of 3600–4500 metres, and descends down to 2100 metres, rarely to 1500 metres, in winter.<ref name=Thrushes/><ref name=China/>

It is a large [[thrush (bird)|thrush]], 28 cm long (same size as a [[mistle thrush]]). The male has a black head, wings, and tail, and a pink body, dark pink below and pale pink to nearly white on the mantle (thus superficially resembling a [[rosy starling]] in plumage pattern, though larger and longer-tailed than that). The female is similar but drabber, with a dark brown head and the pink tinged greyer. Juveniles are similar to adult females, but with pale streaks on the head.<ref name=Thrushes/> It is gregarious, forming flocks particularly in winter, but also on the breeding grounds, and often joins flocks of [[dusky thrush]] and [[red-throated thrush]]. It is omnivorous, feeding on insects, earthworms, and berries, with [[Juniperus|juniper]] cones ("berries") particularly important in winter.<ref name=Thrushes/><ref name=China/>

zh.extract:
棕背黑头鸫（学名：Turdus kessleri）为鸫科鸫属的鸟类，俗名克氏鸫。分布于印度以及中国大陆的西藏、甘肃、青海、四川、云南等地，主要生活于在西藏栖息于3600-4500米林线以上的杜鹃和柳树灌丛中以及冬季下迁至海拔2100米处。该物种的模式产地在甘肃。
